qa_pairs.question: Which NAMD version and module were used for the CPU benchmarking results presented?
qa_pairs.answer: NAMD 2.12 from the verbs module was used for the CPU benchmarking results.

qa_pairs.question: How is efficiency calculated for the NAMD CPU benchmarking?
qa_pairs.answer: Efficiency is computed using the formula: (time with 1 core) / (N * (time with N cores)).

qa_pairs.question: What was the wall time per step for NAMD 2.12 when using 1 core?
qa_pairs.answer: The wall time per step was 0.8313 seconds with 1 core.

qa_pairs.question: What was the efficiency for NAMD 2.12 when running on 1 core?
qa_pairs.answer: The efficiency was 100% when running on 1 core.

qa_pairs.answer: The wall time per step was 0.4151 seconds with 2 cores.

qa_pairs.question: What was the efficiency for NAMD 2.12 when running on 2 cores?
qa_pairs.answer: The efficiency was 100% when running on 2 cores.

qa_pairs.question: According to the benchmark table, at which number of cores did NAMD 2.12 achieve its highest efficiency?
qa_pairs.answer: NAMD 2.12 achieved its highest efficiency of 107% when running on 4 cores.

qa_pairs.question: At what number of cores did the efficiency of NAMD 2.12 first drop below 100%?
qa_pairs.answer: The efficiency of NAMD 2.12 first dropped below 100% when running on 64 cores, reaching 98%.
